key_insight: RUFNet improves few-shot brain tumor segmentation by refining noisy support masks and fusing predictions with uncertainty-aware, query-guided priors using a Hybrid Mamba backbone.
clinical_relevance: Better segmentation robustness could support more reliable tumor delineation for downstream neuro-oncology imaging workflows, especially when labeled examples are scarce.
method: Hybrid Mamba interaction backbone for efficient support-query feature exchange with linear complexity.
Attention-Guided Mask Refinement (AGMR) uses query features to recalibrate noisy support masks and improve prototype consistency.
Uncertainty-Aware Posterior Fusion (UAPF) estimates pixel-wise variance and adaptively balances few-shot predictions with query-aligned priors.
evidence_signal: On BraTS 2020, the model achieved Dice scores of 84.3% in 1-way 1-shot and 86.1% in 1-way 5-shot settings, outperforming compared state-of-the-art methods.
watch_item: Performance is reported on a specific benchmark, so generalization to other scanners, tumor subtypes, and clinical settings still needs validation.
paper_title: RUFNet: Query-Guided Support Mask Refinement and Uncertainty Fusion based on Hybrid Mamba for Few-Shot Brain Tumor Segmentation
